title: Align bridge-zephyr board config filenames with Zephyr board targets
description: Final review for project-LICHEN-2067.1.1 found bridge board config filenames such as firmware/bridge-zephyr/boards/heltec_lora32_v3.conf do not match actual Zephyr board targets such as heltec_wifi_lora32_v3/esp32s3/procpu. Hardware-style no-flash builds can silently skip board configs and fail to exercise CONFIG_LORA/CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 unless EXTRA_CONF_FILE/DTC_OVERLAY_FILE is passed explicitly. Audit bridge-zephyr board conf/overlay names, add/rename target-qualified files where appropriate, and add no-hardware build coverage that proves LoRa/L2 configs are merged for at least one real board target. Evidence: reviewer reproduced heltec_wifi_lora32_v3/esp32s3/procpu not merging heltec_lora32_v3.conf, leaving CONFIG_LORA off.
notes:
Implementation: added target-qualified bridge board fragments for heltec_wifi_lora32_v3_esp32s3_procpu, t_deck_esp32s3_procpu, and t_echo_nrf52840; removed obsolete alias fragments heltec_lora32_v3.*, lilygo_tdeck.*, and lilygo_techo.*; added Python static/gated tests for target-qualified fragment presence, obsolete alias absence, and Heltec real-target auto-merge; annotated lichen_hal return_device_if_ready with __maybe_unused to eliminate board-dependent unused-helper warning. T1000-E was intentionally excluded because project-LICHEN-zte2.3.1 blocks LR1110 no-hardware builds.
Validation local: python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py => 10 passed, 3 skipped; cd python && python3 -m ruff check tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py => passed; git diff --check => passed.
Validation EC2: instance i-0743ec95cf00cccac in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-2067-1-1-1-validate. python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py python/tests/firmware/test_build_epoch_cmake.py => 10 passed, 3 skipped, known pytest asyncio_mode warning. LICHEN_RUN_ZEPHYR_SMOKE=1 python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/firmware/test_bridge_zephyr_cmake.py => 8 passed, known pytest asyncio_mode warning. Direct no-flash west builds with SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1717777777 passed for heltec_wifi_lora32_v3/esp32s3/procpu, t_deck/esp32s3/procpu, t_echo/nrf52840, and rak4631/nrf52840; all auto-merged target-qualified fragments and set CONFIG_LORA=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_L2=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1717777777. Memory: Heltec FLASH 213148 B / 8388352 B, iram0_0_seg 47076 B / 329 KB, dram0_0_seg 69448 B / 313 KB; T-Deck FLASH 213304 B / 16776960 B, iram0_0_seg 47076 B / 329 KB, dram0_0_seg 69464 B / 313 KB; T-Echo FLASH 111896 B / 1 MB, RAM 24084 B / 256 KB; RAK4631 FLASH 127284 B / 1 MB, RAM 33108 B / 256 KB. Notable warnings: known pytest asyncio_mode config warning; nRF DTS duplicate unit-address warnings on T-Echo; RAK4631 Zephyr USB CDC log-level warning tracked in project-LICHEN-2067.1.1.1.1.
Code review: Meitner final review found no P0-P3 findings after stale alias deletion; residual risk is broader board auto-merge coverage remains EC2 sweep evidence rather than all in the gated pytest smoke. Mencius final review confirmed the P2 stale-alias footgun is resolved and found no P0-P2 code findings; P3 Beads export/evidence issue addressed by adding these notes and exporting flat files before closure.
EC2 cleanup after validation: ran sync and unmounted /mnt/lichen-zephyr on i-0743ec95cf00cccac; detached vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and waited for state=available; terminated i-0743ec95cf00cccac and waited for terminated; deleted temporary security group sg-0f9c9330171e09df8. Final volume state: available, Attachments=[] .
close_reason: Completed: target-qualified bridge board fragments now auto-merge for real Zephyr board targets; obsolete alias fragments removed and guarded by tests; no-hardware EC2 validation and code reviews recorded in notes.
